title: mentha dryophila
intro: Mentha dryophila, also known as Dryopteris, is a plant species not to be confused with the genus Mentha, but rather a species of the Dryopteris fern family. However, there is no information available on Mentha dryophila, but there is Mentha pulegium, also known as pennyroyal. If we consider the properties of Mentha pulegium, it has been used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include being a natural digestive aid, anti-inflammatory agent, and an antiseptic. Herbal preparations of pennyroyal, such as teas and essential oils, are often used to alleviate digestive issues, reduce fever, and relieve menstrual cramps.
